Mars has a very different climate compared to Earth. It experiences a cold and dry atmosphere, with average temperatures around minus 80 degrees Fahrenheit (about minus 62 degrees Celsius). However, temperatures can vary greatly depending on the location and time of day, sometimes reaching as high as 70 degrees Fahrenheit (20 degrees Celsius) near the equator during the day, but dropping significantly at night.

Mars also has dust storms that can cover large areas and last for days or even weeks, and it has seasons similar to Earth due to its axial tilt, but they are about twice as long because Mars takes longer to orbit the Sun. If you're curious about more specific aspects of Mars or its weather patterns, feel free to ask!
